ingredients.note: per liter of water, for coloring

steps_en: Dissolve the essential oils together with the cinnamon tincture and citric acid in the 3 liters of alcohol.
Add the remaining liquid, mixing carefully.
After three days of rest, color with caramelized sugar and a little cochineal.
300 g (of caramel/coloring mixture) suffice for every liter of water.
notes_en: This appears to be the tail end of a recipe begun on the previous page; the title and full ingredient list are not visible on this page.

ingredients.note: hot solution of 25 g citric acid dissolved in 100 (units, likely cc or grams) of water

steps_en: Combine the Goa arrack, Jamaica rum, unsweetened peach juice, 90° alcohol, rose spirit and syrup.
Prepare a hot solution of 25 g of citric acid in 100 (parts) of water and add it to the mixture.
notes_en: Proportions given for a large batch (totaling roughly 20 liters of base liquids plus syrup).

ingredients.note: 20 to 30 g per liter of boiling water

steps_en: Prepare a good infusion of tea using 20 to 30 g of tea per liter of boiling water.
Meanwhile, cut a lemon into small pieces and place it in a suitable vessel, adding 400 to 500 g of sugar in pieces.
Carefully add a liter of rum so that it does not mix with the tea infusion.
When the rum has warmed somewhat, set it alight and let it burn without stirring until it is close to extinguishing itself spontaneously.
Then stir everything together to obtain an excellent punch.
Prepare in the same way as the Rum Punch.
notes_en: Recipe refers back to the Rum Punch method; no separate ingredient list given.
Recipe simply refers the reader to the Rum Punch recipe above.

title_it: (nota a piè di pagina, senza titolo)

yield.note: from 30 liters of pure raspberry juice; alternative method yields 15 liters

steps_en: Distill carefully and cautiously in the still 30 liters of pure raspberry juice, to collect 20 liters of product.
Alternatively, mix in the still's boiler 2 kg of freshly pressed raspberry pomace with 500 g of magnesia and 5 liters of 85° alcohol, and distill slowly to collect 15 liters of product.
notes_en: This is a footnote continuing from a recipe on the previous page (likely a raspberry liqueur/spirit); the main recipe text is not shown on this page.
